chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Now we are going to start our chapter 2 in HTML in which we will be learning HTML from a different level In this chapter, we will understand the differences between inline and block elements We will talk about semantic tags

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: We will talk about semantic tags The projects we have made like the portfolio we can consider as mini projects We will add some more things to the activities we did We will practice some more questions and learn more concepts

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: We will practice some more questions and learn more concepts The first thing we are going to start with is the difference between inline and block elements We will create all the elements in HTML Whether it is a paragraph element, a heading element, an image element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Whether it is a paragraph element, a heading element, an image element We can classify all these elements into two categories One category is of inline and one category is of block We will first understand the differences between these two and then we will talk about them in detail

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: We will first understand the differences between these two and then we will talk about them in detail In HTML, block elements are the elements that if you place them somewhere they will occupy the entire width, whether they are needed or not Along with this, all our block elements start with a new line

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Along with this, all our block elements start with a new line We can consider it as a cycle If you have created a cycle in any line, then it will occupy the entire line Then you will not be able to create any other cycle on the same line

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Then you will not be able to create any other cycle on the same line Whether the cycle is small or big If you want to create three cycles in an area, then you have to occupy and round three different lines These are the block elements

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: These are the block elements The element that needs a width or not, occupies the entire line In other elements, in HTML, are our inline elements Inline elements occupy the width and the amount of elements needed on the screen

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Inline elements occupy the width and the amount of elements needed on the screen These elements are not necessary to start with a new line If you want to fit multiple elements in the same line and they can be, then they will come in the same line

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: and they can be, then they will come in the same line They do not need different lines We can consider it as a cycle You are getting a single line on a single line on a single road

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: You are getting a single line on a single line on a single road You will create them one after the other These are the inline elements The first cycle that we saw occupying the entire line was a block element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: The first cycle that we saw occupying the entire line was a block element We have given the example of cycles in HTML It means that I have created a heading tag The heading tag that created the element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: The heading tag that created the element Whether you write a small word in it, like I wrote H1 This is how the hello will be printed on the screen But if you want to print anything after this, then you can consider it as a paragraph tag

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: But if you want to print anything after this, then you can consider it as a paragraph tag The paragraph tag will always start from the next line If the heading tag is created, then it occupies the entire line Whether it is as big as the small word, or the big heading

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Whether it is as big as the small word, or the big heading But the width on the entire page is occupied Let's find out whether it is inline or block elements Here we revise once again

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Here we revise once again Inline elements are the same as the required line Block elements occupy the entire line Let's talk about the heading element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Let's talk about the heading element I have already mentioned in the heading element that it is a block element How to verify this? To verify this, we are going to create a heading element in our HTML file

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: To verify this, we are going to create a heading element in our HTML file First, we will create an HTML file I am going to name it as classroom.html My line is created

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: My line is created I will write an exclamation here I will enter it We have already talked about it

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: We have already talked about it This is my entire boilerplate code In the boilerplate code, I will write inline versus block This is the name of my document

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: This is the name of my document I am going to create a heading tag in the body tag I have created my heading tag from h1 I will write in it, Hello World

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: I will write in it, Hello World If I write in this line in h1 In the next line, I am going to write another h1 In which I will write my college

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: In which I will write my college We will save these two and execute it First, Hello World is printed in one line Then, our college is printed in the second line

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Then, our college is printed in the second line So, Hello World only needed this much space But still, it occupied the entire length And it did not let our college fit in the first line

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: And it did not let our college fit in the first line We can inspect it and verify it I will click on it to inspect the element I can see the blue area that highlights here

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: I can see the blue area that highlights here I can see the entire width I mean, my heading 1 is in the entire block And my heading 2 is also in the entire block

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: And my heading 2 is also in the entire block I can verify the heading element here By writing two headings together That both come on different lines

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: That both come on different lines Even though the words are very small Which do not need the entire line But still, the element will occupy the entire line

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: But still, the element will occupy the entire line The next element we are going to talk about Is my paragraph element While writing the paragraph element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: While writing the paragraph element I had already seen that If we want to bring things on different lines For that, we can divide different content

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: For that, we can divide different content Into different paragraphs So, we should get an idea from this That my paragraph element is also a block element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: That my paragraph element is also a block element That is, a paragraph requires How much content is there in the entire width Which is available to it

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Which is available to it We can verify it with HTML What I am going to do is after heading Or we will convert this heading to paragraph

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Or we will convert this heading to paragraph This is my paragraph 1 This heading will become my paragraph 2 Let's do this in shape

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Let's do this in shape And here I am going to do refresh So, this is my first paragraph This is my second paragraph

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: This is my second paragraph Both the tags come on different lines If I want to inspect this By inspecting this, my first paragraph

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: By inspecting this, my first paragraph We can see that the paragraph is highlighting P for the tag This is also a block element Let's talk about the next element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Let's talk about the next element The next element we will discuss is my anchor tag In the HTML, the anchor tag only takes As much as it is necessary

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: As much as it is necessary An anchor tag is an inline element Let's write the anchor tag once I have written two paragraphs in the HTML document

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: I have written two paragraphs in the HTML document And what I am going to do in this paragraph I will make an anchor tag here Google.com

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Google.com And here comes Google.me And we are going to do similar things In the next line

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: In the next line And this will become my Facebook.com And here I will write Facebook.me Let's do this in shape and refresh

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Let's do this in shape and refresh So, Google.me and Facebook.me Both are coming on the same line Even though I have written both in the HTML document

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Even though I have written both in the HTML document I have written in different lines But because it is an inline element It is as much as it is necessary

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: It is as much as it is necessary If I want to write another anchor tag I can write YouTube here Google.com

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Google.com And I can write this Save and refresh So, this third anchor tag is also after this

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: So, this third anchor tag is also after this And after this, the anchor tags will come Until my entire line is not filled And after that, the next line will start

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: And after that, the next line will start This is an example of an inline element You can inspect this too This is my first element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: This is my first element Which is just so much of the area You can see highlighted This is my second element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: This is my second element This is my third element And if we see that the entire line is filled This is for the body tag

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: This is for the body tag So, from here I feel that my body tag is an element But anchor tags are an inline element Next, we are going to talk about the image element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Next, we are going to talk about the image element The image element also takes only as much space As much as my image is required So, the image element is also an inline element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: So, the image element is also an inline element For example, if I talk about a paragraph And I want to insert an image in a paragraph Let's take a paragraph here

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Let's take a paragraph here This is an image And here I am going to insert an image Let's search for orange

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Let's search for orange Orange image And from here I am going to copy the address of this image

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: I am going to copy the address of this image Let's do this, save And now refresh So, even though this image is a little bigger

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: So, even though this image is a little bigger You won't know it But we can inspect it So, the blue highlight on this image

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: So, the blue highlight on this image Is as much as the width of the image If this was a block element It would occupy the entire width

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: It would occupy the entire width Of the image This is an image that I have written And my image is displayed in the same line

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: And my image is displayed in the same line The image is not displayed in the next line If the image was to be displayed in the next line Then it would look something like this

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Then it would look something like this That I would have written after the paragraph tag So, my orange came down And now my paragraph is on top

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: And now my paragraph is on top But because the image is an inline element That is why it was displayed along with the paragraph In fact, if I display two images

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: In fact, if I display two images I can display them Like, I copy an apple image address Let's remove the paragraph tag from here

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: Let's remove the paragraph tag from here And this is my So, this is my first image tag This is my second image tag

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: This is my second image tag And now I am going to refresh my page Both the images appear on the same line So, in this way, my image element

chunks.title: 3_Inline vs Block
chunks.text: So, in this way, my image element Is an inline element

full_text: Now we are going to start our chapter 2 in HTML in which we will be learning HTML from a different level In this chapter, we will understand the differences between inline and block elements We will talk about semantic tags The projects we have made like the portfolio we can consider as mini projects We will add some more things to the activities we did We will practice some more questions and learn more concepts The first thing we are going to start with is the difference between inline and block elements We will create all the elements in HTML Whether it is a paragraph element, a heading element, an image element We can classify all these elements into two categories One category is of inline and one category is of block We will first understand the differences between these two and then we will talk about them in detail In HTML, block elements are the elements that if you place them somewhere they will occupy the entire width, whether they are needed or not Along with this, all our block elements start with a new line We can consider it as a cycle If you have created a cycle in any line, then it will occupy the entire line Then you will not be able to create any other cycle on the same line Whether the cycle is small or big If you want to create three cycles in an area, then you have to occupy and round three different lines These are the block elements The element that needs a width or not, occupies the entire line In other elements, in HTML, are our inline elements Inline elements occupy the width and the amount of elements needed on the screen These elements are not necessary to start with a new line If you want to fit multiple elements in the same line and they can be, then they will come in the same line They do not need different lines We can consider it as a cycle You are getting a single line on a single line on a single road You will create them one after the other These are the inline elements The first cycle that we saw occupying the entire line was a block element We have given the example of cycles in HTML It means that I have created a heading tag The heading tag that created the element Whether you write a small word in it, like I wrote H1 This is how the hello will be printed on the screen But if you want to print anything after this, then you can consider it as a paragraph tag The paragraph tag will always
